content.full_text:
VALLEY INSURANCE PROGRAM, JOINT POWERS AGENCY
448 West Shaw Avenue • Fresno, California 93704 ¥ (209) 229-1800

Merced Community College District
State Center Community College District
West Hills Community College District
Yosemite Community College District
June 29, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Conflict of Interest Code
Valley Insurance Program, JPA

Dear Mr. Imberi:

We have received two objections to filing under our program's
Conflict of Interest Code. Copies are attached for your
review and advice.

Regarding Mr. Hynes letter, we concur that our resident
agents do not make policy or exercise authority over JPA
spending. They have no vote on the board of directors or the
executive committee.

Regarding the CPA, his point is well made that the auditor
acts independently of the JPA. An auditor does not make any
decisions which may affect financial interest.

Your review and advice will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Vash Webster
Vash Webster

VW:mkh

cc: Dr. Craig Blake

cc: Mr. John D. Hynes


A COMMUNITY COLLEGE PROGRAM

GCH
INSURANCE

THE
GCH
GROUP
3iddings, Corby,
hynes, Inc.
lodeslo, CA

June 26, 1990

The Risk Takers
lodeslo, CA

9immerman
nsurance
igency
3aksdaie, CA

Mr. Vash Webster
Valley Insurance Program Joint Powers Agency
448 West Shaw Avenue
Fresno CA 93704

?on Dale
nsurance
igency
upton, CA

Dear Vash:

C. Simminger
nsurance
igency
lanteca, CA

Re:  Conflict of Interest Code

nance
nsurance
onora, CA

Thank you for your memorandum of June 18,
enclosing the final Conflict of Interest Code.

Central
since
nsurance
Iddock, CA

Since I am not a Director, Officer, Partner,
Trustee, Employee, or in any way responsible for
management for Giddings, Corby, Hynes, the current
resident agent, I assume no statement of economic
interest would be applicable in my case.

If I'm incorrect, please advise me.

Best personal regards,

THE GCH INSURANCE GROUP

John D. Hynes

JDH:ekk

RECEIVED
JUN 27 1990

A. MASON RLODGETT

GIDDINGS, CORBY, HYNES, INC.
P.O. BOX 3231 ■ MODESTO, CA 95353-3231 ■ 209/526-3110 ■ FAX 209/521-1620

Partners
Robert L. Linger
Ronald S. Martin
Jim L. Peterson
Gary A. Shrum

June 25, 1990

Valley Insurance Program, JPA
Attn: Vash Webster
448 W. Shaw
Fresno, CA 93704

Dear Vash:

This letter is in response to our firm's receipt of the Valley
Insurance Program, Joint Powers Agency (VIPJPA) Conflict of
Interest Code and our thoughts thereon regarding the inclusion of
the VIPJPA's auditor.

As noted in the appendix to the code, the auditor shall be required to report in all disclosure categories as enumerated in
paragraph III of the text of the code.

I offer the following in response to the inclusion of the
VIPJPA's auditor in any disclosure category of the code:

1. Under the American Institute of Certified Public
Accountants' Code of Professional Conduct, which is
also effective under the State of California, Department of Consumer Affairs, State Board of Accountancy regulations, a certified public accountant
who is engaged to audit the financial statements of
a client shall be independent in fact as well as in
appearance.

2. Maintaining the status of independence includes, but
is not limited to, not holding any investments, real
property interests, loans, gifts, receiving compensation, other than for auditing services rendered,
or holding any position in management or otherwise,
except as in the advisory capacity of auditor.

3. The current literature for standards of reporting by
a certified public accountant on the audited financial statements of a client require the auditors'
report to be entitled, "Independent Auditors' Report".

Santa Maria Building • 575 E. Locust Ave., • Suite 308 • Fresno, Ca 93710
(209) 438-8740 • FAX 438-8746

4. The "Who Must File...." section of the Form 730
instructions, page 1, specifies "......certain posi-
tions which entail the making or participation in
the making of decisions which may effect financial
interests." An auditor does not make any decisions
which may effect financial interests.

Accordingly, I recommend the exclusion of the VIPJPA's auditor
from the filing requirements of Form 730, Statement of Economic
Interests for Designated Employees, under the VIPJPA's conflict
of interest code.

If you have any questions or concerns regarding this recommenda-
tion, please contact me for further discussion.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Jim L. Peterson, CPA

[RECEIVED stamp showing:]
RECEIVED
JUN 21 1990
A. MASON BLODGETT
